rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 27 body uses `practice`(n) ('how your practice changed'); allowed by the AE101 carve-out — earned at M4, fluent M5-M6, and this file's primary use is AE101 M6. No ritual/ceremony in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 quintet (Boris x Martin x Godin x Sutherland x Siilasmaa). Body is concrete, peer-warm, Boris-direct on the timebox; mood is practitioner fluency (M6) and the forward-looking arc-naming fits Risto-can-lead. No atmospheric/negation-callout/self-contradiction issues in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rough-cut drafts for articles.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Article-drafting rule; this is a curriculum exercise file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit original when paraphrasing named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Frameworks riffed on (scaling-of-learning throughline, agent-as-mirror) are credited in the maintainer block; no named practitioner technique is paraphrased into body without credit.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses second person throughout; 'the student' appears only in maintainer block (exempt). No trainer-cue delivery mechanics in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer terms-of-art (agent-as-mirror, scaling-of-learning) stay in the maintainer block; body uses plain mechanical description ('name the arc', 'read across all six modules').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 9:"Dropping is not the soft option; it is a signal your artefacts did not carry the density the read needed."
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Reassurance-shaped construction ('X is not the soft option') defends the drop alternative path — same shape as the 'not a downgrade / no consolation prize' flag. Trust the diagnostic reframe to carry it; consider 'Dropping is a signal your artefacts did not carry the density the read needed.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Reference-artefact authoring rule; not applicable to an exercise body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No practitioner named in body prose; attribution lives only in the maintainer block (exempt).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Punchy lines ('The arc is yours; the note is just the agent helping you see it.') carry no overstated slogan needing a boundary carve-out.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project strategy docs, NOT student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No vendor/marketing/aspirational-defence framing in body; no 'vendor' usage in body region.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body terms (worktree, sub-task, ADR, git refs, M4/M5) are earned by the M6 arc position; nothing lifts vocabulary ahead of where this file sits.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit closer when trimming body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No dangling pronouns or bypass-the-agent punchlines; closer ('That's the read of your six modules from outside the work.') lands clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Recap heading is `**What happened:**` (past) and body narrates past actions ('You ended with...', 'The agent read across... and wrote the arc').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Human->agent verbs stay peer-flat ('Ask the agent to walk them', 'the agent helping you see it'). No bark/order/command/whip.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No corrective-gap framing and no ability-tier pegging in body; pre-prompt checks are absent (none owed for this beat).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Bare M4/M5 (line 15) fine at M6 per the M2+ convention; 'Modules 1-6' full form (line 9) also acceptable. Load-bearing terms (the arc, the loop) are earned by arc position.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Agents 101 SVP-marketing word ban; this is an AE101 software-engineer file where earned engineering terms are in register.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body does not blur memory/context; references concrete artefacts (observations/, CLAUDE.local.md, ADRs).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The save/drop fork and 'if the read runs ten minutes plus, interrupt' are designed forward-moving moves of this beat, not what-if-X-fails troubleshooting; recovery branches live in the maintainer block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompt ends with output destination + 'Show me the note before you save it' (prompt fence, exempt); body imposes no over-specified shape.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module. Compound-shape varies per module — never repeat same Debrief.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview; this is a single arc-naming move with one push-back, distinct from a review->rewrite->report Debrief.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle another module's voice.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Voice matches M6 practitioner-fluency mood; no borrowed mood from another module.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't ask student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Student reads the note for the arc (emotional/sense-making payoff) and pushes back on truth, not manual error-catching.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Self-study simulation rule; this exercise has no simulation persona.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No research callout boxes in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student moves stay in conversation with agent.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forcing function is the prompt (read, push back, save) done with the agent; the interrupt ('enough. just tell me now.') stays in chat. No editor/terminal mechanic step.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body line 15 names the layer explicitly (`CLAUDE.local.md`); prompt fence (exempt) qualifies both team `CLAUDE.md` and personal `CLAUDE.local.md`.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No em-dashes in body region; sentences are short and split.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Bolded lead-ins ('What you do', 'The point', 'What happened') are canonical exercise signposting; no non-canonical doing-section header reads as a passive noun phrase.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No inline markdown links to other curriculum surfaces in body. Code-span paths (task.md, plan.md, observations/) are the student's own working artefacts, not curriculum-surface links.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: By M6 the contested words (skill, agent, memory/observations) are disambiguated upstream; body uses concrete artefact paths and 'sub-task'/'Agent tool' precisely.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body 'the agent' x7 for autonomous work (correct default), 'Claude' x1 (line 19, plan-summary/invocation behaviour). Agent-shaped framing dominates; no 10:0 assistant skew.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No tomorrow/overnight/tonight/morning anchors in body; uses module names and session-relative phrasing ('when the next long task stalls').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in flow.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No pedagogy-aware reflection header or contemplative-pause prefix. 'Quick timebox note' is operational pacing, not a 'sit with it'/'meta-moment' wrapper.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when student has already decided.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No meta-loop where Claude asks the student to name a decision already made; student reads, pushes back, saves.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is default file type for student artifacts across curriculum.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'Save it as <filename>' with a non-.md extension; destination left as ADR/memo/standalone file with no .txt/.docx substitution.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving student forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body lines each advance the move (point, timebox, where artefacts live, the prompt, skim-past, interrupt, read, save, recap). No implementation-detail aside that fails the next-sentence-better test.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print = dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 23 'Read the note.' carries a load-bearing follow-on direction ('That's the read of your six modules from outside the work.') leading into the save/push-back decision; the note IS the cognitive payoff, not a scoreboard being narrated. Within the carve-out, not the banned generic 'read it'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forward callout 'You'll have your agent re-read it when the next long task stalls' (line 25) keeps the student as grammatical subject; no training-as-subject, coach-imperative, or role-gating.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Operational reminders live at moment of use, not only where introduced.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The 'Note' at line 13 restates the operational state at moment of use (same session, Module 5 worktree, no new session) rather than relying on prework memory.
